items.title: Amazon DynamoDB: AScalable, Predictably Performant, and Fully Managed NoSQLDatabase Service
items.note: This one changed how I think about consistency. The idea that availability can be prioritized over consistency in certain systems — and that eventual consistency is a valid engineering choice — was eye-opening. The section on vector clocks and conflict resolution is dense but worth it.

items.title: Firecracker: Lightweight Virtualization for Serverless Applications
items.note: Fascinating look at how AWS built microVMs that boot in under 125ms. The core insight is that you can strip a VM down to just what serverless workloads need and get near-container speed with VM-level isolation.

items.title: Bloom Paradon - When not to use a Bloom Filter
items.note: The paper that introduced Bloom Filters. What clicked for me was the asymmetry — a Bloom Filter can tell you definitely no, but only probably yes. That framing made the whole thing make sense. Came across this while studying system design and now I see Bloom Filters everywhere.
